about.introParagraph1: Project Gutenberg is a volunteer effort to digitize and archive cultural works.
about.introParagraph2: It was founded in 1971 by Michael S. Hart and is the oldest digital library.
about.introParagraph3: Most of the items in its collection are the full texts of public domain books.
about.introParagraph4: The project tries to make these as free as possible, in long-lasting, open formats.
about.missionParagraph1: The mission of Project Gutenberg is to encourage the creation and distribution of eBooks.
about.missionParagraph2: Our goal is to provide free access to books for everyone.
about.missionParagraph3: We digitize and diligently proofread them with the help of thousands of volunteers.
about.missionParagraph4: Project Gutenberg offers over 70,000 free eBooks.
about.missionParagraph5: Choose among free epub books, free kindle books, and more.
about.missionParagraph6: Download them or read them online.
about.missionParagraph8: All our eBooks were previously published by bona fide publishers.
about.missionParagraph9: No fee or registration is required.
about.missionParagraph10: If you find Project Gutenberg useful, please consider a small donation.
about.missionParagraph11: We are a nonprofit organization and rely on donations.
about.missionParagraph12: Your donation will help us continue to provide free access to literature.
about.missionParagraph14: Project Gutenberg is a registered trademark.
about.missionParagraph15: It may not be used to endorse or promote products without permission.
about.missionParagraph16: For more information, visit our website.
